judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and closely comparable, but Report 2 edges ahead by firmly stating the pipeline has no migration step and that the runbook's automatic-migration claim is unsupported, whereas Report 1 only hedged that claim as unverifiable.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... Must be `"false"`. ... `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... Must be `"false"`. ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... Should be removed or set to `""`. ... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK`... either blank the sink or fix the code

report_2.evidence: `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... it should be omitted from the prod tfvars entirely. ... `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... Must be removed ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS`... Must be omitted entirely ... Both keys should be omitted from prod, not toggled. ... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN`... It should not be recreated in prod

report_1.evidence: the production bundle will call `https://staging-api.example.com` for every API request — this value is frozen into the static JS at build time, so it cannot be corrected post-deploy without a rebuild

report_2.evidence: the frontend bundle shipped to production would point at `https://staging-api.example.com`, because there is no separate production build step and no `.env.production` to source a correct value from even if one were added

Nothing in the pipeline runs database migrations. ... nor any step in `deploy.yml` invoking one. Yet `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy" — a claim the committed pipeline doesn't support.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` has the **exact same string**, `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`... The correct fix is changing `infra/prod/main.tf:3` to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"` before applying.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` has the identical string, `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — not updated to `environment:production`... a `deploy-staging` job's OIDC token could potentially assume the *production* deploy role
